expectations.text: Cargo.toml includes burn with features wgpu, train, vision
Model struct uses #[derive(Module, Debug)] without B: Backend generic
model.init() takes &Device, not &B::Device
main.rs has #![recursion_limit = "256"]
Device is created with Device::wgpu(DeviceKind::DefaultDevice)
No Tensor<B, D> style — uses Tensor<D> without backend generic

expectations.text: Conv2d/Linear configs use .init(device) pattern

notes: Skill correctly enforced #![recursion_limit], WGPU device creation, and no-backend-generic style

notes: All assertions passed — baseline also knows WASM deployment well; non-discriminating eval
Eval 3 (wasm-backend) is non-discriminating — both configs score 100%. Assertions may be too easy.
Eval 1: Skill wins by adding #![recursion_limit] that baseline missed — a concrete compiler-error-prevention win.
Eval 2: Baseline wins by mentioning BinFileRecorder migration the skill missed. Need to strengthen the skill on record-format migration.
Skill adds ~5K tokens on average but saves ~5s of time. Tradeoff is net-neutral.
Consider harder assertions for eval 3 (e.g. binary size estimate, DeviceConfig precision, OnceCell pattern).
